videoDescription:

By the end, you will have a deep understanding of the concepts and a great overall big picture of how Docker is used in the whole software development process.

videoTitle: Expert Talk: DevOps & Software Architecture • Simon Brown, Dave Farley & Hannes Lowette • GOTO 2021
videoDescription:
This interview was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen for GOTO Unscripted. #GOTOcon #GOTOunscripted #GOTOcph
https://gotopia.tech

Read the full transcription of this interview here:
https://gotopia.tech/articles/expert-talk-devops-software-architecture

Simon Brown - Author of "Software Architecture for Developers" & Creator of the C4 Software Architecture Model @simonbrown4821 
Dave Farley - Author of "Modern Software Engineering" Continuous Delivery & DevOps Pioneer @ModernSoftwareEngineeringYT 
Hannes Lowette - Head of Learning & Development at Axxes @Belenar82 

RESOURCES
https://youtu.be/D43PlUr1x_E • Alan Kay • GOTO 2021
https://youtu.be/lLnsi522LS8 • Mary Shaw • GOTO 2015
https://youtu.be/5OjqD-ow8GE • Simon Brown • GOTO 2018

DESCRIPTION
As software architecture continues to evolve rapidly, we are constantly confronted with new challenges. Simon Brown, Dave Farley and Hannes Lowette cover some of the recent trends in software architecture touching on terms such as DevOps and how to deal with complexity. They also reference concepts that have stirred debates forever and are still not done right, like bounded context and continuous delivery.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
00:53 Evolution in software architecture
06:16 Managing complexity
14:22 Bounded context
22:00 Problems in our industry
25:00 Continuous delivery done right
28:56 DevOps: just a title?
31:47 Similarities between software architecture & architecture
39:23 Outro

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Simon Brown • Software Architecture for Developers Vol. 2 • https://leanpub.com/visualising-software-architecture
David Farley • Modern Software Engineering • https://amzn.to/3GI468M
Dave Farley & Jez Humble • Continuous Delivery • https://amzn.to/3ocIHwd

videoTitle: Docker Tutorial for Beginners - A Full DevOps Course on How to Run Applications in Containers
videoDescription:
Get started using Docker with this end-to-end beginners course with hands-on labs.

Docker is an open platform for developers and sysadmins to build, ship, and run distributed applications, whether on laptops, data center VMs, or the cloud.

In this course you will learn Docker through a series of lectures that use animation, illustration and some fun analogies that simply complex concepts, we have demos that will show how to install and get started with Docker and most importantly we have hands-on labs that you can access right in your browser.

videoTitle: What is DevOps? - In Simple English
videoDescription:
Simple explanation of DevOps without the complications and information overload.  -- what do you think? Please share & comment! 

DevOps is a concept with different interpretations and definitions, but when you get down to it, it's all about developers and operations teams breaking down silos and working together to innovate faster. For many companies, the ability to innovate at a rapid pace — responding to market conditions and customer feedback — is a key factor for success. Adopting the DevOps philosophy requires a new mindset, new tools and new skills.

More about DevOps:

videoTitle: DevOps Engineering Course for Beginners
videoDescription:
What is DevOps in the context of software engineering? Learn all about DevOps in this comprehensive course for beginners with three technical tutorials. You will learn what DevOps is, continuous integration, continuous deployment strategies, and application performance management. Many DevOps practices are commonly used in programming and web development, it is important to understand key terms and technologies. 

This course often references the MERN (MongoDB, Express JS, React JS, Node JS) technology stack. You will get a series of talks with technology recommendations based on these best practices. There will be several programming examples embedded in these talks. As long as you know the absolute basics of coding and the web, you'll have no problem following along. 

⭐️ Course Notes ⭐️
🔗 Lesson 1: https://layerci.com/blog/what-is-devops/

videoTitle: ChatGPT Tutorial - Use ChatGPT for DevOps tasks to 10x Your Productivity
videoDescription:
ChatGPT Tutorial for DevOps - What is ChatGPT? How to use ChatGPT? My opinion on whether ChatGPT will replace engineers
💛  Follow me on LinkedIn and IG for weekly updates   ►  https://bit.ly/2F3LXYJ
💙  Become a DevOps Engineer - 6-month program       ►  https://bit.ly/43nK7ra
💚  Mini-Bootcamp for IT Beginners                                  ►  https://bit.ly/3BRz6To

#chatgpt   #techworldwithnana 

►  This video is sponsored by Firefly 🙌🏼
►  For more infos: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-Lf0
►  Open source tool AIaC: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-GD0

I'm sure you have all heard of ChatGPT by now. It has become a buzzword within days of its release and professionals in all fields, especially in high skilled areas like lawyers, doctors, engineers are questioning whether such AI can actually replace them and work.

So in this video I want to talk about what ChatGPT is and how it even popped up, talk a bit about the organization behind GPT called "OpenAI", which has already created many other machine learning models besides Chat GPT and also explain technically about all that.
And then we'll dive in and actually put ChatGPT to use for some DevOps related tasks.
I really want to see how it can help in generating configuration code for building DevOps processes or different parts of those processes and how well it knows different DevOps technologies, but not just some shallow examples or boilerplate code that I can get from official documentation, but instead also try more fine-tuning and small optimizations in that configuration code.

We're also going to check out an open source command line tool that is built on top of ChatGPT and was specifically created for engineers to generate infrastructure as code templates and more and finally we'll talk about the impact of ChatGPT, the quality and usefulness of such a tool for engineers and whether it will really replace the engineers and to what extent you should be concerned.

53:53 - Tools built on top of OpenAI's API

01:01:00 - My opinion on ChatGPT & whether ChatGPT will replace engineers

#edureka #DevOpsEdureka #DevOps #DevOpsTools #DevOpsTraining #DevOpsTutorial #DevOpsJenkins

About the Course
Edureka’s DevOps online training is designed to help you master key tools of Devops lifecycle like Docker, Puppet, Jenkins, Nagios, GIT, Ansible, SaltStack and Chef used by a DevOps Engineer for automating multiple steps in SDLC. During this course, our expert DevOps instructors will help you:

1. Understand the concepts and necessities of DevOps
2. Understand the need for DevOps and the day-to-day real-life problems it resolves
3. Learn installation and configuration of common infrastructure servers like Apache, and Nginx for the Enterprise
4. Learn popular DevOps tools like Jenkins, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, SaltStack, Nagios and GIT
5. Implement automated system update, installations and deployments 
6. Learn Virtualization Concepts 
7. Configuration deployment and packaging, continuous integration using GIT
8. Fine tune Performance and set-up basic Security for Infrastructure 
9. Manage server operations using Code which is popularly known as Infrastructure as a Code
10. Understand the need for and concepts of Monitoring and Logging. 

Along with the above mentioned topics, to help you master the most popular DevOps tools, you will also receive 3 additional self-paced courses including presentations, class recordings, assignments, solutions for the following tools:

1: Ansible - Covers Introduction, Setup & Configuration, Ansible Playbooks, 37 Ansible Modules, Different Roles and Command Line usage. 2: Chef - Covers Introduction, Building the Cook Book, Node Object & Search, Data-bags, Chef environment, Roles, Deploying Nodes in Production and using the Open Source Chef Server.

3: Puppet - Covers Puppet Infrastructure & run-cycle, the Puppet Language, Environment defining Nodes and Modules, Provisioning a Web Server and Executing Modules Against A Puppet Master.

Who should go for this course?
DevOps practitioners are among the highest paid IT professionals today, and the market demand for them is growing rapidly. With emergence of new job roles around DevOps philosophy, anyone aspiring to get into these new roles, can take up this DevOps course. Some of these roles are:

1. DevOps Architect
2. Automation Engineer
3. Software Tester
4. Security Engineer
5. Integration Specialist
6. Release Manager

Project Work
1. Host a dummy webpage using Apache Web Server.
2. Write shell script which reports:
a) Various system configurations related to the user and the OS.
b) Data related to load on the server.
c) Top 5 processes with maximum number of threads.
d) Sort the services by memory
3. Install Nagios on a VM node for monitoring the various parameter of the VM.

videoDescription:

You will learn about several DevOps tools (Git, Jenkins, Docker, Puppet, Ansible, Nagios) involved at different DevOps stages such as version control, continuous integration, continuous delivery, continuous deployment, continuous monitoring. #Edureka #EdurekaDevOps #DevOpsTutorial #DevOpsTools #DevOpsTraining #Git #Jenkins #Docker #Puppet #Nagios #DevOpsUseCase

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

About the Course
Edureka’s DevOps online training is designed to help you master key tools of Devops lifecycle like Docker, Puppet, Jenkins, Nagios, GIT, Ansible, SaltStack and Chef used by a DevOps Engineer for automating multiple steps in SDLC. During this course, our expert DevOps instructors will help you:

1. Understand the concepts and necessities of DevOps
2. Understand the need for DevOps and the day-to-day real-life problems it resolves
3. Learn installation and configuration of common infrastructure servers like Apache, and Nginx for the Enterprise
4. Learn popular DevOps tools like Jenkins, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, SaltStack, Nagios and GIT
5. Implement automated system update, installations and deployments 
6. Learn Virtualization Concepts 
7. Configuration deployment and packaging, continuous integration using GIT
8. Fine tune Performance and set-up basic Security for Infrastructure 
9. Manage server operations using Code which is popularly known as Infrastructure as a Code
10. Understand the need for and concepts of Monitoring and Logging. 

Along with the above mentioned topics, to help you master the most popular DevOps tools, you will also receive 3 additional self-paced courses including presentations, class recordings, assignments, solutions for the following tools:

1: Ansible - Covers Introduction, Setup & Configuration, Ansible Playbooks, 37 Ansible Modules, Different Roles and Command Line usage. 2: Chef - Covers Introduction, Building the Cook Book, Node Object & Search, Data-bags, Chef environment, Roles, Deploying Nodes in Production and using the Open Source Chef Server.

3: Puppet - Covers Puppet Infrastructure & run-cycle, the Puppet Language, Environment defining Nodes and Modules, Provisioning a Web Server and Executing Modules Against A Puppet Master.

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

Who should go for this course?
DevOps practitioners are among the highest paid IT professionals today, and the market demand for them is growing rapidly. With emergence of new job roles around DevOps philosophy, anyone aspiring to get into these new roles, can take up this DevOps course. Some of these roles are:

1. DevOps Architect
2. Automation Engineer
3. Software Tester
4. Security Engineer
5. Integration Specialist
6. Release Manager

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

Project Work
1. Host a dummy webpage using Apache Web Server.
2. Write shell script which reports:
a) Various system configurations related to the user and the OS.
b) Data related to load on the server.
c) Top 5 processes with maximum number of threads.
d) Sort the services by memory
3. Install Nagios on a VM node for monitoring the various parameter of the VM.

videoTitle: The ONLY Roadmap to Get a DevOps Job in 2026 | Zero to Hero Guidance | Senior DevOps Engineer | USA
videoDescription:
This is the exact roadmap that will get you hired in 2026. 🚀

Stop following random roadmaps that leave you more confused than when you started. The DevOps landscape is shifting, and simply knowing a few tools isn't enough anymore. To land a high-paying role today, you need to understand the logic, the architecture, and the "why" behind every deployment.

I’m Avi (Vamsi Avinash Gunji). I’ve spent years navigating the enterprise tech world—from my first role at Clarivate in 2019 to roles at Farmers Insurance, The Home Depot, PwC, and Goldman Sachs, leading up to my current position as a Senior DevOps Engineer. I’ve seen what actually works in production and what’s just "tutorial noise."

In this video, I’m breaking down the definitive path I would follow if I were starting from zero today. We’re debugging your career "Desi style"—from code errors to chai breaks. This is my narrative—unfiltered, evolving, and unfinished.

📍 The Roadmap Breakdown:
In this video, we cover the critical phases to master:
✅ Phase 1: Mastering the Linux terminal, systemctl, and Bash/Python automation.
✅ Phase 1.2: Why Networking (DNS/VPCs) and Storage (NFS) are your best friends.
✅ Phase 2: Cloud architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP) vs. the reality of Private Cloud and On-Prem.
✅ Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform & Ansible) to save you from disasters.
✅ Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes (K8s)—The core of modern engineering.
✅ Phase 4.2: Building actual end-to-end CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins and GitHub Actions.
✅ Phase 5: Monitoring, Alerting, and Observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk).

🕒 TIMESTAMPS:

00:00 Intro
00:41 Phase 1: Linux Foundations
01:40 Phase 1.2: Networking and Storage
02:48 Phase 2: The Cloud Ecosystem
04:02 Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code
04:57 Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes
06:18 Phase 4.2: CI/CD Pipelines
06:47 Phase 5: Monitoring & Alerting
07:28 Conclusion: Senior Tip


🔗 Official Resources & Certifications:
Linux Foundation (LFCS): https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/linux-foundation-certified-sysadmin-lfcs/ 

AWS DevOps Engineer Certification: https://aws.amazon.com/certification/certified-devops-engineer-professional/

Kubernetes (CKA) Certification: https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/certified-kubernetes-administrator-cka/

Official Terraform Site: https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform

Official Helm Charts: https://helm.sh/docs/

videoTitle: Cloud Engineer vs DevOps Engineer - Differences and Overlaps of tasks and responsibilities
videoDescription:
Differences and Overlaps of Cloud and DevOps Engineer Roles as well as deep dive of Cloud Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

DevOps engineer and Cloud engineer are 2 popular roles in software engineering. But there is a lot of mix up of these two roles and many people and many companies often use them interchangeably. They mix the tasks and responsibilities of these two very often. For example there are plenty of job descriptions for "DevOps Cloud Engineer" or "Cloud DevOps Engineer", which would naturally make you think that they are the same role, but then why would you have two names for the same role? And that's exactly what I'm going to discuss and clarify in this video.


🔗💡  What is DevOps and DevOps Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

videoTitle: What is Platform Engineering and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
videoDescription:
Platform Engineering - What it is, how it works and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
Also how to approach implementing an IDP - internal developer platform.

🔗💚   Become a DevOps Engineer in 6 months       ►   https://bit.ly/3WKUPGf
🔗💙   Become a Kubernetes Administrator              ►   https://bit.ly/3IMJUWI
🔗🧡   Complete GitLab CI/CD course                        ►   https://bit.ly/42itb4B
🔗 💜  DevSecOps Bootcamp                                       ►   https://bit.ly/3OI6y66

▬▬▬▬▬▬ Thank you Pulumi for sponsoring this video 👏  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
►   Learn more about Pulumi's Internal developer platform-in-a-box: https://pulumip.us/IDP-Platform-Engineering
►   Join PulumiUp - a global Infrastructure as Code conference: https://www.pulumi.com/pulumi-up/

In this video we're going to talk about a relatively new hot topic in DevOps and Cloud space, which is “platform engineering”. There is a lot of discussion going on, where some people are asking whether platform engineering replaces DevOps. Many people say it goes hand in hand and is rather an addition to DevOps, but in reality it's a bit more complicated than that. Platform engineering actually changes a lot of established rules we knew about DevOps, SRE and Cloud engineering, it changes the game and introduces some new rules.

So let's clearly define, what platform engineering exactly is, more interestingly, why was there even a need for this new role and how it evolved and of course how it compares to DevOps and Cloud engineering and does it really replace any of these roles?


▬▬▬▬▬▬ T I M E S T A M P S ⏰  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
0:00 - Intro and Overview
0:50 - Why Platform Engineering? Issue in traditional and DevOps teams
08:30 - Standardize usage of tools
09:09 - Non-functional requirements
10:55 - What Platform Engineering is responsible for?
15:47 - How Platform Engineering works? Internal Developer Platform (IDP)
17:53 - How it looks in real-world?
22:33 - Flexibility with Infrastructure as Code
24:30 - How to implement an IDP successfully
30:14 - Platform Engineering vs DevOps
34:53 - Both Skills in TWN education
39:33 - Platform vs Cloud Engineer
41:01 - Wrap Up

videoTitle: Entendendo "Devops" para Iniciantes em Programação (Parte 1) | Série "Começando aos 40"
videoDescription:
Finalmente chegamos no tema final da série! Vamos falar um pouco sobre o tal do "devops".

Esta parte meio que depende dos conceitos que vimos nos últimos episódios então se você é iniciante, e ainda não assistiu os anteriores, recomendo que faça isso.

No episódio de hoje quero dar um pouco do contexto histórico, dos anos 90 até os anos 2000, indo de configuração manual de máquinas, hospedagens compartilhadas, virtualização até os VPS. Vamos entender como uma coisa foi levando pra próxima.

Muita gente confunde muitos termos, virtualização, paravirtualização, jails, containers, hypervisors e não sabe o que é o que, então eu vou distinguir tudo pra vocês finalmente entenderem.

videoTags: what is devops in simple english
channelDescription:
GitHub is the AI-powered developer platform to build, scale, and deliver secure software. We make it easier for developers to be developers: to work together, to solve challenging problems, and to create the world’s most important technologies. We foster a collaborative community that can come together—as individuals and in teams—to create the future of software and make a difference in the world.

The GitHub platform is where developers can create, share, and ship the best code possible. Our community is made up of students, hobbyists, consultants, enterprise professionals, partners, and executives, building software in the way that works best for them.

GitHub is much more than code. It is the home of open-source collaboration. It is where new developers get started and where experienced developers expand their knowledge. It is a community where developers come together to solve the unsolvable and test the limits of what software can do.

videoTitle: How AI is Changing DevOps Careers | What You Need to Know
videoDescription:
DevOps Career in 2026: Should You Learn AI or Double Down on DevOps Fundamentals?
► Grab your DevOps Starter Kit: https://bit.ly/4oZ4qGD
► Skills Companies ACTUALLY Want: https://youtu.be/7d3MU2DgUPg

In this video I sat down with Bret Fisher - Docker Captain and DevOps educator who spent 2 years walking around conferences asking engineers one question: Are you actually using AI in DevOps?

The disconnect between what people say online and what's really happening will surprise you.

We break down:
- the AI hype vs reality
- what AI can actually do for DevOps RIGHT NOW (not in 5 or 10 years)
- and the practical first steps you can take without getting overwhelmed by the chaos

Plus: why companies still want Kubernetes experts, not AI prompt engineers.
If you're confused about AI in DevOps, this conversation will clear everything up.

videoTitle: DevOps Interview Questions & Answers! (How to PASS a DevOps Engineer Interview!)
videoDescription:
DevOps Interview Questions & Answers by Richard McMunn of: https://passmyinterview.com/devops-engineer-interview/
#devops #devopsengineer #devopsinterviewquestions
In this interview tips training tutorial, Richard McMunn will cover:

1. A list of DEVOPS interview questions I recommend you prepare for;
2. Example ANSWERS to help you PASS your DevOps Engineer interview;
3. The QUALITIES you MUST demonstrate during your DevOps interview;
4. Further resources to help you PASS your DevOps Engineer interview!

HERE'S A FULL LIST OF DEVOPS ENGINEER INTERVIEW QUESTIONS
Q1. Tell me about yourself and why you want to work in this DevOps role?
Q2. What are the most important skills and qualities needed to work as a DevOps Engineer?
Q3. Why do you want to work for our organization in this DevOps role?
Q4. Tell me about the hardest day you’ve had as a DevOps Engineer?
Q5. What are your strengths and weaknesses?
Q6. You are giving a presentation to a number of non-technical company managers. They have no technical knowledge and they are struggling to understand your presentation. How would you adapt your presentation, so they all understand?
Q7. Where do you see yourself in the future?
Q8. How do you handle stress and pressure at work?
Q9. What does DevOps mean to you and what is its purpose in our organization?
Q10. Tell me how DevOps differs from Agile / SDLC?
Q11. What is an inode on Linux?
Q12. In your opinion, which are the top DevOps tools and what are they used for?
Q13. Tell me how you'd go about optimizing a database?
Q14. What is garbage collection programming?
Q15. Talk me through the steps you would follow when checking physical memory on Linux?
Q16. Explain how you copy a local file to HDFS?
Q17. Describe how you would make a background process run?
Q18. Assuming there are no cookies, how would you make your application work?
Q19. Talk me through the process of what happens when someone accesses a URL via a web browser?
Q20. What is the difference between CI and CD?
Q21. Tell me how you would measure network packet?
Q22. What is a distributed cache in Hadoop?
Q23. There are a large number of requests due to high demand. How would you ensure your database works?
Q24. Why do you want to leave your current job?
Q25. What are your salary expectations in this DevOps Engineer role?
Q26. That’s the end of your DevOps interview, do you have any questions for the panel?

Connect with Richard McMunn on LinkedIn.com:
https://www.linkedin.com/in/richard-mcmunn-coach/

Download Richard's 26 DevOps Engineer Interview Questions and Answers guide in PDF:
https://passmyinterview.com/devops-engineer-interview/

videoTitle: Deploying to on-premises Windows machines with Azure DevOps - Part 1 | DevOps Lab
videoDescription:
In this series, Abel and Zachary Deptawa walk through deploying to on premises Windows servers behind firewalls using Azure DevOps.

In part one, we walk through configuring a Build for a .NET application inside of Azure DevOps. In later episodes, the build will be used with Deployment Groups on Windows servers and we'll set up a Release Pipeline to repeatably and reliably deploy to those Windows servers behind a firewall.

Follow Zachary Deptawa on Twitter: @zdeptawa
Follow Abel Wang on Twitter: @abelsquidhead

channelDescription:
This is the Microsoft channel for videos related to the various DevOps tools, practices and services. Subscribe to learn more about the vast Microsoft technologies such as Azure DevOps, GitHub & GitHub Actions, cloud native technologies, all while delivering greater DevOps capabilities in Azure! 

DevOps is not only a product but a practice, a culture and a mindset. Most importantly, it is for everyone! DevOps allows you to plan, execute and iterate your work in a way that delivers a higher quality product to your customer and increases value to your entire organization. DevOps methodologies enable collaboration and transparency. Here we’ll deliver content on the various technologies that enable DevOps on Azure.

There are various upcoming companies who have leveraged AWS cloud solutions for their growth. AWS was the first company which started to provide cloud solutions on a large scale. Those new to the Programming and DevOps world often find it challenging to start their journey due to the missing basic prerequisites. For example, issues with setting up a basic lab environment using VirtualBox, problems with networking, or trouble with working with the Linux CLI or text editors like vi editor. Sometimes there are issues with applications - like getting a sample application to work, problems with getting the dependencies to install correctly or trouble with getting a web server to communicate with a database server. Or, at other times, it's working with data formats like JSON or YAML.

So we have identified a gap, and we believe that this is due to missing some of the basic prerequisites, such as knowing the basics of Linux, the basics of tools like VirtualBox, or knowing the basics of the most commonly used applications like Java, Python or NodeJS or web servers like Apache, NGINX etc. As a DevOps or Cloud Engineer, it is crucial to have these basics cleared. And that’s why we are helping you to bridge that gap!

videoTitle: Introducing AWS DevOps Agent | Amazon Web Services
videoDescription:
AWS DevOps Agent is a frontier agent that resolves and proactively prevents incidents, optimizes application reliability and performance, and handles on-demand SRE tasks across AWS, multicloud, and on-prem environments. It investigates incidents  and identifies operational improvements as an experienced DevOps engineer would: by learning your applications and their relationships, working with your observability tools, runbooks, code repositories, and CI/CD pipelines,  and correlating telemetry, code, and deployment data across all of them. Ask questions, get instant contextual answers, and create custom charts and reports that you can save and share with your team.

videoTitle: Jenkins Full Course in One Shot | Zero to Pro CI/CD (6+ Hours) + 3 Real Projects | DevOps Tutorial
videoDescription:
#theshubhamgour 

🚀 Welcome to the Ultimate Jenkins CI/CD Course — in ONE SHOT!
This 6+ hour hands-on tutorial takes you from Zero → Pro in Jenkins with 3 real DevOps projects. Perfect for beginners, Release Engineers, DevOps aspirants, and software teams who want to automate builds, testing, and deployments efficiently.

✔ Jenkinsfile from scratch — Stages, Agents & Steps
✔ Jenkins Shared Libraries for production workflows
✔ GitHub → Jenkins Webhooks automation
✔ Multi-Branch Pipelines for real DevOps environments

2️⃣ QA Automation pipeline triggered on commits

DevOps & Cloud beginners

Students preparing for job interviews

Anyone learning automation for career growth

videoTitle: DevOps Project: Mastering VPC Creation with Terraform & GitHub Actions Automation
videoDescription:
► ▬▬▬▬▬▬ About🧾 ▬▬▬▬▬▬
In this tutorial video, we'll walk you through creating a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) infrastructure using Terraform and then automating the deployment of your Terraform code using GitHub Actions. Whether you're new to infrastructure as code (IAC) or looking to streamline your deployment workflow, this video covers all of it.🚀

Here's what you can expect to learn:
1. Setting up Terraform: We'll guide you through the initial setup of Terraform on your local machine, ensuring you have the necessary tools and configurations in place.🛠️

2. Writing Terraform Code: You'll see how to write Terraform code to define your VPC infrastructure, including networks, subnets, security groups, and more. We'll provide clear explanations and best practices along the way.📝

3. Initializing and Applying Terraform: Learn how to initialize your Terraform project and apply your code to create the VPC in your cloud provider.🌟

4. GitHub Repository Setup: We'll guide you through setting up a GitHub repository to store your Terraform code, including creating a README, .gitignore, and other essential files.📂

5. GitHub Actions Configuration: Discover how to configure GitHub Actions workflows to automate the deployment of your Terraform code. We'll cover secrets management and workflow triggers on pushing the code to a specific branch.⚙️

6. Push to GitHub: See how to push your Terraform code to your GitHub repository and trigger the GitHub Actions workflow.📦

7. Monitor and Verify: Learn how to monitor the progress of your GitHub Actions workflow and verify the successful deployment of your VPC infrastructure.🧐

By the end of this video, you'll have a solid understanding of how to create and manage infrastructure using Terraform and how to automate the deployment process with GitHub Actions. Don't forget to like, subscribe, and hit the bell icon to stay updated with more exciting tutorials and tech content. Let's dive in and empower your infrastructure automation journey!💪📺

videoTitle: What Is DevOps? | How To Implement? | Benefits of Implementing DevOps | Malayalam
videoDescription:
What is DevOps? | How To Implement? | Benefits of Implementing DevOps | Malayalam

Introducing DevOps is becoming a major steps in any new age software development life cycle. This video will give you what is DevOps and how to implement DevOps in each phases of Software Development Cycle such as 

1. Planning phase
2. Coding Phase
3. Build Phase
4. Continuous Testing 
5. Continuous Deployment 
6. Continuous Monitoring 
7. Continuous Integration 

Also discussing why an organization should introduce DevOps approach and the various benefits they could achieve by rolling out ‘DevOps’. The key benefits are 

•Ensure faster deployment.
•Stabilize work environment.
•Significant improvement in product quality.
•Automation in repetitive tasks leaves more room for innovation.
•Promotes agility in your business.
•Continuous delivery of software.
•Fast and reliable problem-solving techniques 

Finally a brief intro about waterfall model and the importance of switching to agile approach in software development and DevOps is an extension to the agile approach.

videoTitle: What is Azure DevOps? | How to Use Azure DevOps

videoTitle: DevOps ที่แท้จริงเป็นยังไง? | Career Lab EP.1 (Part 1)
videoDescription:
ทำไมแต่ละองค์กรถึงให้นิยามความหมายของ DevOps ไม่เหมือนกัน แม้กระทั่ง JD ของอาชีพ DevOps Engineer แต่ละที่ยังแตกต่างกันอย่างสิ้นเชิง

ถ้า DevOps คือแนวคิด ไม่ใช่ตำแหน่งงานแล้ว จะหยิบจับใครมาเป็น DevOps Engineer ก็ได้จริงหรือ? 🤫

ร่วมฟังความคิดเห็นจาก 2 มุม 2 สายอาชีพ 👨🏻‍💼 ตัวแทนทีม Software Developer โดยคุณปุ๋ย สมเกียรติ ปุ๋ยสูงเนิน - Technical Coach ที่สยามชำนาญกิจ  👨🏻‍💼 และตัวแทนทีม Operation หรือ DevOps Engineer, SRE, Platform Engineer โดยคุณเดียร์ จิรายุส นิ่มแสง - ผู้ก่อตั้ง และ CEO ของ Opsta (Thailand)

รายการ Career Lab รายการที่สำรวจและเจาะลึก insight ต่าง ๆ ของอาชีพที่ in-demand ที่สุดในตอนนี้ เราจำนำพา Expert ตัวท้อปขมานั่งเม้าท์ฉ่ำกับประเด็นสุดร้อนแรงแบบไม่มีกั๊ก และไม่เคยพูดถึงที่ไหนมาก่อน !!

✅ กว่า 70% ของ DevOps Engineer ในปัจจุบันไม่ใช่ DevOps ที่แท้จริง?
✅ DevOps (อาจ) ไม่มีอยู่จริง จริง ๆ แล้วคือ Platform Engineering
✅ องค์กรจะปรับตัว Transform ในการทำ DevOps ที่แท้จริงได้อย่างไร?
✅ โลกของ DevOps การทำงานจริง ใครควรรับผิดชอบอะไร ทั้งในมุมของทีม Software Dev และทีม DevOps Engineer, SRE, หรือ Platform Engineer?
✅ Team Lead หรือ Tech Manager ควรจะแก้ปัญหา ความไม่เข้าใจกันระหว่างทีมพัฒนาในองค์กรอย่างไร?

💡 อย่าลืมกด Subscribe และ เปิด 🔔 แจ้งเตือนเพื่อไม่ให้พลาด Contents ดีๆ จาก Skooldio

videoTitle: How to configure DevOps for Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations and connect with Visual Studio
videoDescription:
The video demonstrates how to configure DevOps for Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations and connect with Visual Studio. Beginning from the creation of a DevOps free cloud service to the creation of the DevOps project and repository folders for the Main branch, the training shows then to Visual Studio and connect to the DevOps project. Then there are various steps to map the local Main and Dev branch folders to the remote repository on the DevOps service. The successful strategy of Main and Dev branches is discussed in the background of sandbox and production instances via LCS and how the strategy is successful. Additionally, a sample D365 VS project is created and demonstrated the check-in, and then how to replace any previous check-in versions from history.

videoTitle: Hot Tips For Implementing SLOs #devops
videoDescription:
Struggling to implement SLOs? In this video we break down a practical, step-by-step path: pick user-centric Service Level Objectives, define SLIs, set realistic targets, use error budgets to balance reliability,  and switch to burn-rate alerts to kill noisy paging. 

What you’ll learn: 

🧩 Why SLOs are customer-focused goals 

🧩 How to choose core user journeys and the right SLIs 

🧩 Setting realistic SLO thresholds and establishing an error budget

🧩 Using budget burn-rate alerts so you only wake up when users feel pain

🧩 Avoiding common pitfalls: no baseline, complex tooling, error-budget confusion, microservices overload

Try Chronosphere: Modern observability built for cloud-native scale. See how Chronosphere helps teams define, measure, and act on SLOs.

videoTitle: New 📚 Release! Architecting and Implementing DevOps for Infrastructure Management in Azure #books
videoDescription:
New 📚 Release! Architecting and Implementing DevOps for Infrastructure Management in Azure: Infrastructure DevOps in Azure: Configure, Deploy, & Automate Your Azure resources with terraform, GitHub Actions and Ansible by Ashish Raj

Embark on a transformative journey from basic to intricate infrastructure configurations—think high availability, scalability, and security—all through the power of Infrastructure as Code. Dive into the automation of Azure resource deployments, leveraging DevOps tools with comprehensive, step-by-step guidance and practical examples. But the learning doesn't stop at deployment; delve deeper into automated governance and monitoring, ensuring your infrastructure is not just built but also managed with precision and efficiency.

Find it on Leanpub!

#MicrosoftAzure #coding #programming #newreleases #books #bookstan

Link: https://leanpub.com/devopsinazure

About the Book

In the face of accelerated development cycles, Infrastructure teams must align with the culture of rapid deployment and infrastructure versioning. Embracing Infrastructure as Code, Continuous Integration, and Configuration Management practices becomes imperative. Tools like Terraform, GitHub Actions, and Ansible empower infrastructure engineers to implement scalable Infrastructure as Code practices in Azure.

This book serves as a thorough guide for deploying, configuring, and automating Azure infrastructure using DevOps tools. It navigates through cloud architecture patterns with DevOps tools, illustrating their application in Azure solution design. Subsequently, the book delves into the implementation of the architected solution, utilizing DevOps tools like Terraform, GitHub Actions, Packer, Ansible, etc., to address specific architectural requirements with emphasis on scaling the solution architecture and establishing governance, aligning with DevOps principles.

Upon finishing this book, readers will be equipped with the expertise to transform business requirements into Azure-compatible solution architectures, seamlessly incorporating a DevOps approach to deployment. Readers will demonstrate proficiency in executing end-to-end deployment with governance and monitoring proficiently through the consistent application of DevOps tools.

About the Author

Ashish Raj is a technologist and storyteller who helps engineering teams improve their velocity. He believes the biggest challenges facing engineers aren't technical, but not knowing how to tie people, process and technology. With an extensive background spanning innovative startups to leading tech conglomerates, Ashish currently serves as a Cloud & DevOps Architect at Haufe Group.

A fervent advocate and contributor to the DevOps and cloud communities, he shares expertise through webinars and local meetups. Holding Microsoft certifications in DevOps, Architecture, and Certified Microsoft Trainer. Ashish co-founded "AzureTalk," a prominent IM-based global Azure community on Telegram, and initiated "AzureDevOpsPro," a thriving DevOps community. He shares educational cloud and DevOps videos on his YouTube channel, youtube.com/ashishrajsrivastava

videoTitle: DevOps Myths Everyone Gets Wrong!
videoDescription:
A lot of people think they understand DevOps but so many still get it wrong 😅.

In this short video, I’m breaking down the biggest DevOps misconceptions that confuse developers, IT teams, and tech leads everywhere.

If you’ve ever heard someone say “DevOps is just about tools” or “we’ll just hire a DevOps engineer and boom, we’re DevOps” — this one’s for you.

I’ll explain:
- Why DevOps isn’t about tools like Docker, Kubernetes, or Jenkins
- Why you can’t outsource DevOps culture
- And how DevOps connects developers and operations to work faster, smarter, and with fewer surprises.

Whether you’re new to DevOps or already deep in the cloud-native world, this short will help you finally understand what DevOps really is — and what it’s not.

👉 If you found this helpful, like the video, subscribe, and drop a comment telling me which DevOps myth you’ve heard the most.

videoTitle: How Top Teams Monitor Infrastructure in 2026 #DevOps #Grafana
videoDescription:
Prometheus is powerful—but Prometheus alone isn’t enough. In modern DevOps and cloud environments, collecting and storing metrics is only half the job. 

You also need clear, real-time visualization to fully understand what’s happening inside your complex systems. That’s where the Prometheus + Grafana combo becomes absolutely essential for any team aiming for efficiency. 

Prometheus is responsible for collecting and storing crucial system metrics such as CPU usage, memory consumption, response time, and error rates. It works efficiently using time-series data and triggers alerts when specific thresholds are exceeded, ensuring that you can act quickly when issues arise. 

However, raw metrics alone can be challenging and difficult to interpret at scale, especially in large environments. This is where Grafana steps in to solve this problem by transforming Prometheus metrics into beautiful, interactive real-time dashboards that are easy to read and understand. 

With Grafana, DevOps teams can visualize trends, detect anomalies faster, and monitor the overall health of their infrastructure at a glance, making it far easier to manage resources. 

This powerful combination is widely used in cloud-native applications, Kubernetes orchestration, and enterprise-level DevOps environments across various industries. 

Together, Prometheus and Grafana help teams move from reactive troubleshooting to proactive monitoring and observability, making it easier to identify potential issues before they escalate. 

That’s precisely why this stack is a standard choice for a wide range of professionals, including DevOps engineers, Site Reliability Engineers (SREs), and cloud architects who are on the front lines of technology. If you’re learning DevOps or currently working with monitoring tools, understanding how Prometheus and Grafana work together is an invaluable must-have skill that can greatly enhance your capabilities. 

👉 Comment “Grafana” if you want a full video explaining dashboards, metrics, and real-world setups, including tips and best practices. 

👉 Follow for more bite-sized DevOps and monitoring content that can help you stay up to date in this fast-evolving field. 

Ref: https://prometheus.io/

videoTitle: CPU Spike at 3 AM? Here's What Catches It #devops #shorts
videoDescription:
Monitoring tools don’t have to be complicated.

In this short video, monitoring tools are explained in the simplest possible way, so even beginners can understand them easily. Think of monitoring tools as health trackers for servers and applications. Just like a smartwatch tracks your heartbeat, monitoring tools track the health of your systems.

Prometheus plays a key role in modern DevOps monitoring. It continuously checks system metrics such as CPU usage, memory, response time, and error rates. This constant monitoring helps teams understand how healthy their infrastructure is at any moment.

When something feels wrong—like a sudden spike in CPU usage or a drop in performance—alerts automatically fire. This allows DevOps teams to act quickly, often fixing issues before users even notice. That’s why monitoring is essential for cloud platforms, corporate systems, retail applications, and large-scale infrastructure.

This video is perfect for DevOps beginners, IT students, cloud engineers, and anyone new to monitoring tools. Simple explanations build strong foundations, and understanding monitoring early will help you grow faster in your DevOps journey.

After a merger, things can get messy fast.

For us, LaunchDarkly became the way to get control back, especially once real customers were on the line.

It’s not about hoping changes work. It’s about knowing you can flip a switch, roll things back, and stay in control when production gets unpredictable.
channelDescription:
LaunchDarkly is the runtime control layer for the AI era. For over a decade we have helped engineering teams control what ships in production, from feature flags to full release governance. Today that mission extends to the AI agents and code that are increasingly building, modifying, and operating software on behalf of engineering teams.

With CodeControl and AgentControl, teams can ship faster, govern what runs in production, and automatically respond when something needs to change, without waiting on a redeploy. LaunchDarkly runs on infrastructure that handles over 50 trillion flag evaluations a day, which means the same platform that governs your features also governs your agents.

Founded in 2014 by Edith Harbaugh and John Kodumal, LaunchDarkly has been named on the Forbes Cloud 100 list and serves some of the most demanding engineering teams in the world.

Learn more at launchdarkly.com

videoTitle: From Security Alerts to Automated Action Rethinking DevSecOps in the Age of AI
videoDescription:
#DevSecOps #Cybersecurity #AI #SoftwareDevelopment #DevOps #Automation #CloudSecurity #EngineeringLeadership

Chapters:
0:00 Introduction
2:23 BIMP Security
7:11 Operationalizing at Scale
12:36 Influence of AI-driven World
16:30 Thanks

Security teams today are overwhelmed with alerts.  But too often, those alerts don’t lead to meaningful action.  As software development accelerates and AI becomes embedded into engineering workflows, organizations need a new approach to DevSecOps.  An approach that connects risk directly to automated outcomes.

In this episode, Michael Roberts, VP of Sales and Marketing at SPK and Associates, sits down with Hannah Foxwell, co-founder of BIMP.ai, to explore how security is evolving in an AI-driven world.

In this conversation, they discuss:

Why traditional security tools create alert fatigue instead of real risk reduction

How BIMP is connecting security insights directly to automated action

The growing importance of “golden images” and embedding security into development workflows

How organizations can operationalize security at scale without slowing down engineering teams

What modern software development looks like in an AI-first world

Whether you’re leading DevSecOps initiatives, managing engineering teams, or rethinking your security strategy, this discussion provides practical insights into building more secure, automated, and scalable systems.

If you’re looking to modernize your DevSecOps approach, SPK and Associates can help you align tools, processes, and security practices to drive real outcomes.

ABOUT SPK AND ASSOCIATES

videoTitle: InFocus - Honeycomb: Accept Code is Never Going to Be Perfect #LaunchDarkly #DevOps #FeatureFlags
videoDescription:
I used to be on call 24/7 as a team lead, no vacations, no unplugging. All because of one big lie: that software could be perfect before it hits production.

Reality? It never is.

What actually matters isn’t perfect code. It’s having control. The ability to roll back instantly, fix fast, and adapt when things go wrong.

That’s the shift: from chasing perfection to building confidence in change.
channelDescription:
LaunchDarkly is the runtime control layer for the AI era. For over a decade we have helped engineering teams control what ships in production, from feature flags to full release governance. Today that mission extends to the AI agents and code that are increasingly building, modifying, and operating software on behalf of engineering teams.

With CodeControl and AgentControl, teams can ship faster, govern what runs in production, and automatically respond when something needs to change, without waiting on a redeploy. LaunchDarkly runs on infrastructure that handles over 50 trillion flag evaluations a day, which means the same platform that governs your features also governs your agents.

Founded in 2014 by Edith Harbaugh and John Kodumal, LaunchDarkly has been named on the Forbes Cloud 100 list and serves some of the most demanding engineering teams in the world.

Learn more at launchdarkly.com

videoTitle: FinOps Cost Optimization saves 2400 dollars |  For DevOps SRE Interviews
videoDescription:
FinOps Project | AWS Cost Optimization | Cloud Cost Optimization | EKS Cost Optimization | RDS Cost Optimization | Kubernetes Cost Optimization | CloudZero Tutorial | MCP Server | Codex AI | AWS FinOps | DevOps Cost Optimization | SRE Real World Project | AWS Cost Saving Strategies | CloudWatch Metrics | FinOps Engineer

In this video, I share a real-world update from an ongoing FinOps and AWS Cost Optimization project where we are actively reducing cloud costs across AWS services, including EKS, RDS, Lambda, and S3. This is not a theoretical tutorial. These are actual cost optimization activities being performed in a production environment and discussed with engineering leadership during weekly FinOps review meetings.

You'll learn how we are using AI-powered tools, MCP Server integrations, Codex, CloudZero, and CloudWatch metrics to identify overprovisioned resources and generate actionable recommendations that help reduce AWS cloud spending.

What You'll Learn in This Video

✅ Real-world FinOps project updates from an SRE perspective
✅ How AI tools can analyze AWS CloudWatch metrics
✅ EKS pod resource optimization using CPU and Memory Requests & Limits
✅ Kubernetes cost optimization strategies for Dev, QA, and Production environments
✅ How to identify overprovisioned workloads in AWS EKS
✅ AWS RDS cost optimization recommendations
✅ Reducing RDS instance size, storage, and IOPS costs
✅ Multi-AZ RDS optimization for non-production environments
✅ Using CloudZero and MCP integrations for cloud cost visibility
✅ FinOps reporting, Jira tracking, and savings documentation
✅ How engineering teams validate cost optimization changes safely

Real Cost Savings Discussed
In this project, we identified opportunities to:

Reduce Kubernetes pod resource allocations
Right-size AWS RDS instances
Optimize RDS storage and IOPS
Remove unnecessary Multi-AZ deployments in development environments
Track monthly and annual savings through FinOps processes

These optimizations can result in significant annual cost savings while maintaining application performance and reliability.

✅ The hidden costs of siloed ALM, PLM, DevOps, and quality systems
✅ Real examples of integration challenges in aerospace, medical device, and regulated industries
✅ How disconnected tools create duplicate work, poor visibility, compliance risk, and slower innovation
✅ Why flexible integrations matter in multi-vendor engineering environments

✅ The role of OpsHub integrations and SPK’s engineering expertise in building scalable digital thread solutions

videoTitle: Why DevOps Engineers Should Learn AI Infrastructure
videoDescription:
Why DevOps Engineers Should Learn AI Infrastructure (MLOps, AI Agents & Kubernetes)

Most DevOps engineers today are learning Kubernetes, CI/CD, observability, cloud platforms, and platform engineering.

But another major shift is happening underneath all of it: AI Infrastructure.

Behind every ChatGPT response, AI Agent, RAG application, recommendation engine, and LLM-powered system is a complex infrastructure stack involving GPUs, Kubernetes, model serving, inference systems, vector databases, observability, and distributed systems.

The interesting part?
Many of the skills required to build and operate these systems already overlap with what DevOps engineers have been doing for years.

In this video, you'll learn:

✅ Why AI Infrastructure is growing so rapidly
✅ Training vs Inference explained
✅ Why inference is the real production workload
✅ GPUs and Kubernetes for AI workloads
✅ Model Serving and AI Platforms
✅ RAG, Embeddings & Vector Databases
✅ AI Observability and Monitoring
✅ AI Agents as Distributed Systems
✅ ML Engineer vs MLOps vs AI Infrastructure Engineer vs Platform Engineer
✅ Why DevOps Engineers are uniquely positioned for this opportunity

▬▬▬▬▬▬▬ Timestamps ▬▬▬▬▬▬▬

00:56 - AI Changed Infrastructure Forever

05:28 - Why DevOps Engineers Have an Advantage

07:04 - Why AI Observability Is Hard
07:46 - AI Agents Are Distributed Systems
09:16 - ML Engineer vs MLOps vs AI Infra vs Platform Engineer

Series: From DevOps to AI Engineering

In this series, we'll explore AI from a systems engineering perspective, including:

• AI Infrastructure
• MLOps & LLMOps
• AI Agents
• Kubernetes for AI
• GPUs & Model Serving
• Vector Databases

If you're a DevOps, Platform, SRE, Cloud, or Infrastructure Engineer looking to understand where AI is heading, this series is for you.

videoTitle: Integrating DDI into DevOps: API-First Design
videoDescription:
In this video, GigaOm Field CTO Jon Collins discusses the importance of integrating DDI (DNS, DHCP, and IP address management) into DevOps pipelines using EfficientIP’s  API-first design philosophy.

You’ll learn about:
*Enabling Automation: DevOps and infrastructure-as-code practices rely on automated provisioning and configuration to accelerate deployment.
*Programmable Network Services: For these practices to be effective, core network services like DDI must be programmable.
*EfficientIP’s API-First Integration: By exposing the full functionality of the platform through APIs, SOLIDserverTM DDI services can be integrated directly into automation workflows and DevOps pipelines.
*Streamlining Deployment: This allows DNS records, resources, and policies to be created as part of the same deployment process for an application or service.
*Operational Benefits: By moving DDI directly into the pipeline and eliminating separate manual steps, EfficientIP helps organizations:
**Deploy infrastructure faster.
**Reduce manual errors.
**Maintain consistency across environments.
**Reduce operational burden on teams.

Want to learn more about the DDI market landscape, its evolution, emerging features, and key capabilities?
